name: Fair Housing Council of Riverside County, Inc.- Palm Springs
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit providing free fair housing counseling, landlord-tenant mediation, homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention services across Riverside County since 1986.
description_long:
Fair Housing Council of Riverside County, Inc. (FHCRC) is a non-profit organization established in 1986 and approved by the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD). The organization's primary mission is to protect housing rights and combat unlawful housing discrimination across Riverside County. FHCRC works directly with government offices to ensure Fair Housing laws are upheld and serves as a critical resource for residents facing housing-related challenges.

FHCRC offers a comprehensive suite of free services including anti-discrimination counseling, landlord-tenant dispute resolution, first-time homebuyer workshops (with scheduled classes July 2025-June 2026), foreclosure prevention assistance, pre-purchase consulting, and credit counseling. The organization also provides training programs and maintains a resource library with educational materials on protected class definitions and tenant rights. A key service is their active intervention in housing disputes, as demonstrated by documented client successes.

The organization distinguishes itself through direct advocacy and mediation work—going beyond passive education to actively intervene on behalf of clients with property managers and landlords. FHCRC maintains multiple communication channels (phone, email, social media) and employs named counselors (such as Craig and Juanita) who provide personalized assistance. The organization also addresses systemic barriers by offering bilingual resources (Spanish-language materials) and maintaining current COVID-19 and eviction prevention resources.

FHCRC is best suited for renters facing discrimination or repair disputes, first-time homebuyers seeking education before purchase, individuals at risk of foreclosure, and those needing credit counseling. The primary limitation is geographic—services are specific to Riverside County. Additionally, while the website lists credit counseling as a service, the depth and scope of that offering is not detailed. As a free non-profit service, availability may depend on funding and staff capacity.
pros: HUD-approved organization with 38+ years of experience specifically in fair housing enforcement
Free services with no hidden fees—anti-discrimination counseling, landlord-tenant mediation, and foreclosure prevention assistance
Active client advocacy: documented success resolving disputes (full security deposit recovery, landlord accountability for repairs)
Comprehensive first-time homebuyer program with scheduled workshops throughout 2025-2026
Bilingual resources available (Spanish-language eviction prevention and moratorium materials)
Named, accessible counselors (staff listed by name) providing personalized assistance
Covers broad range of fair housing issues: discrimination, disability access, familial status, source of income, sexual orientation
cons: Geographic limitation: services are restricted to Riverside County only
Credit counseling listed as service but lacks detailed information about scope, certification, or availability
No online booking system visible—RSVP requires phone contact (may create barriers for some users)
Website does not provide response times, wait periods, or capacity information for counseling services
Limited detail on foreclosure prevention program specifics or success rates
best_for: Riverside County renters facing discrimination, unsafe housing conditions, or landlord disputes
First-time homebuyers seeking free education and pre-purchase counseling before obtaining a mortgage
Homeowners at risk of foreclosure seeking prevention assistance and credit counseling
Individuals needing documentation of discrimination or fair housing violations for legal purposes
diagnosis: FHCRC is ideal for Riverside County residents needing free fair housing advocacy, landlord-tenant mediation, or homebuyer education. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Riverside County and reliance on phone contact for appointment scheduling, which may delay access for those without reliable phone service.
services: California Eviction Moratorium information (English and Spanish)
Protected class rights education and materials
Tenant rights and housing law consultation
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 190 W Amado Rd, Palm Springs, CA 92262
meta_description: HUD-approved non-profit providing free fair housing counseling, landlord-tenant mediation, homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention.
